strengths: 13+ years of full-stack experience spanning Node.js, TypeScript, React, and multiple backend languages
Current AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Strong engineering craftsmanship background: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Docker/Kubernetes
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience mentioned in the resume
Limited evidence of Kotlin; Java appears only in older robotics/mobile orchestration roles
No mention of Vitest specifically (testing experience cited generally, not framework-named)
response.justification: Candidate clearly meets the 6+ years bar with a deep full-stack profile across TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, and Docker/Kubernetes (Optilyz, RIDE, GISCollective). The role explicitly requires AI agent development, and the current re:cinq role directly cites MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a strong, rare match. Engineering rigor is evidenced by TDD/CI/CD at RIDE and a long teaching/workshop record. Gaps are narrow but real: Nest.js and Vitest are not named, and Kotlin/Java exposure is dated. Given the framework gaps are learnable for a senior with this breadth, an interview is warranted to validate Nest.js fluency and recent Kotlin appetite.
